title: ISO/IEC 27001 A.8.2 — Cisco ISE Privileged Admin Role-Change Evidence

compliance.assurance_rationale: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.8.2 requires control of privileged access rights. UC-17.1.71 produces continuous machine-readable evidence of every ISE admin role grant, change, and revocation.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrate that ISE admin privilege changes are logged, attributable, and reviewable — covering admin role grant/change/revoke events plus any super-admin elevation.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.6.57' running on `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog`, archived to `audit_evidence` with `iso27001_clause=A.8.2` tagging.

app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263) (Splunkbase) + Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services (1915).
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (60050, 60051, 60052, 60100) earliest=-1d
| eval iso27001_clause="A.8.2", control="Privileged_Access"
| table _time, admin_user, admin_role, admin_action, source_ip, iso27001_clause
| collect index=audit_evidence
description: We continuously evidence ISO 27001 A.8.2 by capturing every ISE admin role change as audit-quality evidence.
value: ISO auditors require evidence of privileged-access lifecycle. This UC turns operational ISE admin events into compliance evidence with attribution (who changed what, when, and from where).
implementation: Daily schedule. Forward-only — never delete or mutate the audit_evidence index entries.
visualization: A.8.2 evidence panel: role-change timeline; super-admin grant gauge.

references.title: UC-17.1.71 — Cisco ISE Admin Account Lockout, Failed-Login Burst, and Privileged-Role Audit

knownFalsePositives: 1. Bulk role updates during planned admin reorg — annotate the change ticket reference in the alert payload.
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- UC-17.1.71 operational.
- `audit_evidence` index with 3y+ retention.

## Step 1 — Data collection

Via Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services (1915).

## Step 2 — Implement

```ini
[UC-22.6.57: ISO 27001 A.8.2 — ISE Admin Role Evidence]
cron_schedule = 0 6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.6.57,reg=ISO27001,clause=A.8.2
```

## Step 3 — Validate

Confirm `index=audit_evidence uc=22.6.57` populates daily.

## Step 4 — Operationalize

Build a compliance dashboard panel titled 'ISO 27001 A.8.2: ISE Privileged Access' showing: (1) Role-change timeline; (2) Super-admin grant audit table; (3) A.8.2 control status single-value.

## Step 5 — Audit

Monthly evidence pack export with control-owner attestation.
grandmaExplanation: We continuously evidence ISO 27001 A.8.2 by capturing every ISE admin role change. That is important for the audit, where proof needs to line up with what the rules expect.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Grant an admin a super-admin role. UC-22.6.57 logs the event in audit_evidence with A.8.2 tagging within 5 min.
controlTest.negativeScenario: No role changes. Empty result set.

evidence: Saved search results archived to `audit_evidence` with ISO 27001 A.8.2 tagging; monthly evidence pack PDF/CSV exports.
